privileged_info_summary: Task uses fully synthetic data created by setup_task.sh via odfpy library. Main_Inventory sheet contains exactly 42 items with mixed price formats. Family_Promises sheet contains 16 items with exactly 4 items promised to multiple family members (conflicts): Antique Oak Dresser (John and Sarah), Quilt Collection (Mary and Elizabeth), Piano (Sarah and Michael), Telescope (David and Robert). Sentimental_Keep sheet contains exactly 8 items marked as non-sellable: Wedding Album, Military Medals, Photo Albums, Grandmother's China Set, Grandfather Clock, Piano, Quilt Collection, and Jewelry Box. Note that Piano and Quilt Collection appear in both the conflict list and sentimental list, creating double restrictions. Verifier logic correctly expects all 4 conflicts and all 8 sentimental items. All task.json fields verified: id='estate_sale_organizer@1', version='1.0', difficulty='medium', timeout=240 seconds, max_steps=15, task uses pre_task and post_task hooks, success via verifier.py::verify_estate_sale_organizer.

audit_notes.metadata_field_status: ABSENT - task.json does not contain a 'metadata' field. All claims verified from source code analysis rather than from declared metadata.
audit_notes.data_generation: Synthetic data created by setup_task.sh lines 19-145 using Python odfpy library. All items, values, and conflicts are hardcoded in the script.
audit_notes.data_verification_method: Source code analysis of setup_task.sh and verifier.py
